name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 08.09.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 08.09.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 05 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1105.42 lakh ha as compared to 1078.49 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 100.8 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 05 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1105.42 lakh ha as compared to 1078.49 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 100.8 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal in East India, NE India, Chhattisgarh, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and South Karnataka .
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal in East India, NE India, Chhattisgarh, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and South Karnataka .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 07 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 9% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 37%; (iii) Central India: 11%; and South Peninsula: 08%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 07 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 9% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 37%; (iii) Central India: 11%; and South Peninsula: 08%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The  Deep  Depression  over  North  Gujarat  and  adjoining  Southwest  Rajasthan  moved slowly  west-northwestwards.  It  is  very  likely  to  move  nearly  westwards  as  a  deep depression across southeast Pakistan and adjoining Rajasthan till 8 th afternoon. Thereafter,  it  will  continue  to  move  nearly  westwards  and  weaken  gradually  into  a depression.
texts.text: The  Deep  Depression  over  North  Gujarat  and  adjoining  Southwest  Rajasthan  moved slowly  west-northwestwards.  It  is  very  likely  to  move  nearly  westwards  as  a  deep depression across southeast Pakistan and adjoining Rajasthan till 8 th afternoon. Thereafter,  it  will  continue  to  move  nearly  westwards  and  weaken  gradually  into  a depression.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over Rajasthan and Gujarat state till 08 th  September with  possibility  of  Extremely  heavy  falls  at  isolated  places  over  Rajasthan  &  south Gujarat, also exceptionally heavy falls ( ≥ 30 cm) over north Gujarat region and Saurashtra & Kutch.
texts.text: Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over Rajasthan and Gujarat state till 08 th  September with  possibility  of  Extremely  heavy  falls  at  isolated  places  over  Rajasthan  &  south Gujarat, also exceptionally heavy falls ( ≥ 30 cm) over north Gujarat region and Saurashtra & Kutch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated to scattered rainfall likely over the entire country.
texts.text: Isolated to scattered rainfall likely over the entire country.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  04 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 158.39 billion cubic meter, which is 87% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 106.8 percent of last year's storage and 121.9 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  04 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 158.39 billion cubic meter, which is 87% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 106.8 percent of last year's storage and 121.9 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Green Gram (White Grub) & (Tobacco Caterpillar) in Rajasthan and  for  Soyabean  (Girdle  Beetle)  in  Maharashtra  and  (Soyabean  Yellow  Mosaic  Virus)  in Madhya Pradesh. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Green Gram (White Grub) & (Tobacco Caterpillar) in Rajasthan and  for  Soyabean  (Girdle  Beetle)  in  Maharashtra  and  (Soyabean  Yellow  Mosaic  Virus)  in Madhya Pradesh. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (28 Aug -03 Sept 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 48%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  03 rd Sept  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 8%.
texts.text: During the week (28 Aug -03 Sept 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 48%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  03 rd Sept  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 8%.

texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The  monsoon  trough  now  passes  through  centre  of  deep  depressionover  the  Southeast Pakistan and adjoining Rajasthan & Kutch, Churu, Agra, Lucknow, Praygraj, Daltonganj Digha and thence eastwards to northeast Bay of Bengal.
texts.text: The  monsoon  trough  now  passes  through  centre  of  deep  depressionover  the  Southeast Pakistan and adjoining Rajasthan & Kutch, Churu, Agra, Lucknow, Praygraj, Daltonganj Digha and thence eastwards to northeast Bay of Bengal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be normal to above normal over east & northeast India; near normal over central & south Peninsular India and below normal over most parts of northwest India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be normal to above normal over east & northeast India; near normal over central & south Peninsular India and below normal over most parts of northwest India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil  moisture  as  on  03  September  2025  was  similar/better  than  the  average  of  the  past  9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  West Bengal, major parts of Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Kerala, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil  moisture  as  on  03  September  2025  was  similar/better  than  the  average  of  the  past  9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  West Bengal, major parts of Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Kerala, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in northern parts of Bihar , in  pockets of eastern part  of  UP,  in  parts  of  Assam,  in  parts  of  Saurashtra  region  of  Gujarat,  in  pockets  of southern parts of Odisha.
texts.text: It was lower than average of past 9 years in northern parts of Bihar , in  pockets of eastern part  of  UP,  in  parts  of  Assam,  in  parts  of  Saurashtra  region  of  Gujarat,  in  pockets  of southern parts of Odisha.
